Lander style description: Editorial minimal — warm cream, oxblood accent, restrained traditional layout. The baseline reference design.
Acid-yellow page with thick black borders, hard offset shadows, oversized uppercase declarative headlines, mono labels.
NYT/Substack-style long-form manifesto with drop cap, pull quotes, multi-column body, byline metadata, italic-accented serif headline.
Demographic Targeting: Everyone, slight lean to scholarly / long-form-reader audiences

Lander style description: Chrome metallic headline over pink/cyan/violet gradient, fake OS-window form (titlebar + min/max/close), starfield, sparkles. Deliberately over-the-top.
Demographic Targeting: STRICTLY 40+ (goofy nostalgia test — gauge whether the era-specific aesthetic pulls older audiences)

Lander style description: Full-bleed saturated color blocks (Bauhaus poster). Vibrant primaries (red / yellow / blue / lime), no shadows, no rounded corners, chunky uppercase display sans.
Green-on-black CRT phosphor aesthetic. JetBrains Mono throughout, scanline overlay, blinking cursors, CLI-prompt form (fake $ prompts + > inputs), ASCII rules.
Demographic Targeting: CS niches ONLY: computer science, cyber security, programming, frontend / backend dev, Linux, devops, sysadmin, etc.
